channel.title: Life Cycle Assessment on RaihnForge
channel.description: Recent content in Life Cycle Assessment on RaihnForge

item.title: Sustainability Proposal: A Life Cycle Assessment Study of Secondary-Use Cases for Cellular Devices
item.description:
Abstract

There is a growing concern regarding sustainability in the domain of technology devices (Paska, 2010). Notably, cellular devices have increased consumption rates and disposal at end-of-life disposition pathways, especially the integrated circuit (IC) and display components (Ercan et al., 2016). Often, devices are destined for landfills, adding to toxic decomposition. Even attempts to recycle or remanufacture still require a great deal of energy and resources (Pamminger et al., 2021). Sustainable practices with well-designed product life cycle accountability considerations would reduce material and energy waste and remove harmful disposition pathway results. One way to affect the rate of new production and consumption of devices would be to create a secondary-use phase for the use phase in the product life cycles.

item.title: Key Sustainability Concepts: Life Cycle Thinking, LCA, LCCA, Triple Bottom Line, and Circular Economy
item.description:
Key Sustainability Concepts

It is imperative to understand the key sustainability concepts of life cycle thinking, life cycle assessment, life cycle cost analysis, the triple bottom line, and circular economy in order to enable sophisticated and impactful sustainable decision-making.

Life Cycle Thinking

What is Life Cycle Thinking

Life Cycle Thinking is the beginning of consideration of a manmade product or service informed through the lens of natural life cycles for the purpose of gathering information about obtaining all materials to produce, manufacture, and use in order to educate and enact sustainable system designs (Matthews et al., 2014). Sustainability, ecological footprint, and product life cycle understandings are essential for making sustainable choices for product life cycles such as cellular phone devices. It establishes systems thinking and boundaries to define and manage elements.

item.title: LCA Article Analysis: Modelling of Different Circular End-Of-Use Scenarios for Smartphones
item.description:
LCA Article Analysis

This document is intended to be reviewed by relevant stakeholders pertaining to sustainability impacts in conjunction with the circular end-of-use scenarios for smartphones. The information presented in this document is oriented to a life cycle assessment (LCA) practices approach that complies with the International Organization for Standards (ISO) 14040-44. The document will summarize, with special regard to ISO LCA Study Design Parameters, the peer-reviewed journal article Modelling Of Different Circular End-Of-Use Scenarios For Smartphones (Pamminger et al., 2021). Specifically, the study’s methods, LCA relevant summary and analysis, and recommendations are noted.
